title: quercus humilis f. leptobalanus
intro: Quercus humilis f. leptobalanus, also known as the dwarfed sessile oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus humilis species, characterized by its small size and distinctive leaf shape. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Quercus humilis f. leptobalanus include its ability to support the immune system, aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
